title: Completion of the Large Hadron Collider (LHC)
description: The Large Hadron Collider, the world's largest and highest-energy particle accelerator, was completed by CERN in collaboration with over 10,000 scientists worldwide.

title: James Webb Space Telescope Launch
description: Webb launched on December 25, orbiting the Sun at the second Lagrange point (L2) to study every phase of cosmic history, from the Big Bang to the evolution of the Solar System.

title: Aristotle's Contributions to Science
description: Aristotle wrote widely on scientific subjects and laid the foundations for much of the work that followed, though he relied entirely on thought and argument without conducting experiments.

title: Galileo Disproves Aristotle's Falling Objects Theory
description: Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei disproved Aristotle's assertion that heavier objects fall faster than lighter ones, introducing the importance of empirical evidence.
